rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY MURRAY ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Petitioners To Compel Nonparty To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2025, Line 10 (Part 1 of 2 of the tentative ruling). PETITIONERS JOHNNY MURRAY, BENJAMIN CASTRO, AND RYLEE LIZCANO's Motion To Compel Nonparty To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents. The court grants Petitioners' unopposed request to compel further production of documents in response to subpoena by third party Episcopal Community Services of San Francisco. The Berger Declaration establishes that ECS represented it would produce documents responsive to RFPs 7-9 of Petitioners' subpoena. After repeated representations that further documents were forthcoming, ECS has not produced further documents and has equivocated about whether further responsive documents exist. (Berger Dec. paras. 19-28.) The court orders ECS to produce documents responsive to RFPs 7-9, consistent with its representations of production in meet and confer correspondence, within 25 days of entry of this order. If ECS has no further responsive documents, it shall provide a verification so attesting. Petitioners do not seek sanctions, and the court awards none here. Independent of the merits of this motion, the court notes that Petitioners have placed into the public Register of Actions an exhibit containing individual reasonable accommodation requests by shelter residents. (See Berger Dec. Ex. 6.) Some of the requests disclose confidential or sensitive medical and mental health information. The exhibit is unnecessary to the court's disposition of this motion and risks invasion of privacy of shelter residents, who are not parties to this case and have not placed their private information at issue. The court admonishes Petitioners' counsel to be mindful of the private information they receive in discovery from third parties in this case, and to consider with care whether that information needs to be filed with the court. With respect to this issue, the court orders as follows. The court orders the clerk to seal the Berger Declaration. The court finds that there exists an overriding interest in protecting the medical and mental health privacy rights of shelter residents as described herein that overcomes the right of public access to this declaration; that this overriding interest supports sealing the Berger Declaration and replacing it with a new version of the Berger Declaration, as described in the next paragraph; that a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; that sealing the current version of the Berger declaration and replacing it with a new version as directed in the following paragraph is narrowly tailored; and that no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest in protecting the medical and mental health privacy of shelter residents. The court further orders Petitioners to submit for filing a new public version of the Berger Declaration that omits Exhibit 6 or states that it is sealed pursuant to this court's order within five court days of entry of this order. (End of part 1 of 2 of the tentative ruling). =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to Contra Costa County** MARIANA GREUB ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two From Defendant American Honda Motor Co., And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2025, Line 11. PLAINTIFF MARIANA GREUB, AND ESTON GREUB's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two From Defendant American Honda Motor Co., And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiffs' motion to compel responses to requests for production of documents, set two, from defendant American Honda Motor Co. is denied. The parties have submitted competing declarations about whether or not service of these documents was made. Neither set of declarations is more persuasive than the other. Because it is Plaintiffs' burden to show entitlement to relief, the court resolves the deadlock against Plaintiffs, but they may re-serve the discovery to obtain responses. The court finds that both parties acted with substantial justification and no sanctions are awarded. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ENERSPONSE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. SMARTRENT.COM, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT SMARTRENT TECHNOLOGIES,INC.'s Motion To Seal. The unopposed motion of Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Enersponse, LLC ("Enersponse") for an order maintaining under seal certain portions of Defendant/Cross-Complainant SmartRent Technologies, Inc.'s ("SmartRent") Motion for Summary Judgment ("Motion"), specifically portions of SmartRent's: (1) Memorandum of Points and Authorities in support of the Motion ("Memorandum"); (2) Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in support of the Motion ("Separate Statement"); and (3) Declaration of James Maxwell Cooper in support of the Motion, including Exhibits G - M, O, and P attached thereto, and Exhibit Nos. 49, 50, 51 to James McPhail's deposition (Exhibit P) (collectively, the"Confidential Exhibits") is granted. The court orders as follows: The exhibits presently lodged by SmartRent provisionally under seal in connection with its summary judgment motion are hereby permanently sealed. Enersponse shall attach Exhibit 1 to the declaration of Marcel Budiono to the written order it prepares for this court so that those lesser redacted versions of Exhibits O and P to the Copper Declaration, filed in connection with SmartRent's summary judgment motion, shall be placed in the public court file with the filing of this order. The court finds that, for the reasons set out in Enersponse's moving papers, supported by declaration showing the proprietary and confidential nature of the materials affected by this order, (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. ROBERT W. GARDNER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Ma Tiers Admitted And Documents Genuine Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2033.280 For The Failure To Respond The Request For Admission Of Facts And Documents, And Request For Sanctions Pursuant C.C.P. Section
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2025, Line 12. 2 - PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And Documents Genuine Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2033.280 For The Failure To Respond The Request For Admission Of Facts And Documents, And Request For Sanctions Pursuant C.C.P. Section. Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, to defendant Robert W. Garner is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by Defendant, as is the genuineness of any document attached. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions but does not find the amount sought to be reasonable. The court awards $1500 in sanctions to Plaintiff, payable by Defendant Robert W. Garner. Defendant shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. ROBERT W. GARDNER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Discovery: Interrogatories-Special Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2030.290 And Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2030.290 And Section 2023.010
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2025, Line 12. 1 - PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion To Compel Response To Discovery: Interrogatories-Special Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2030.290 And Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2030.290 And Section 2023.010. Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc.'s motion to compel Defendant Robert W. Garner to respond to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One, is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a).) Defendant is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, within 10 court days of entry of this order. Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court does not find that Defendant acted with substantial justification but does not find the amount of sanctions sought to be reasonable. The court awards $1500 in sanctions to Plaintiff, payable by defendant Robert W. Garner, within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MITCHELL D. BOULWARE VS. ABC TILE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2025, Line 13. 2 - DEFENDANT LOWE'S HOME CENTERS, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Lowe's Home Centers, LLC seeks an order striking from Plaintiff's complaint his statement that he may seek relief for other stone slab products that caused his injury in the course of discovery. The court denies Lowe's motion to strike as to these allegations. Plaintiff's allegations are proper under Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, 80-81, which approved similar language. Lowe's attempts to distinguish Bockrath on the grounds that that case involved manufacturers and not a retailer like Lowe's, but that distinction is immaterial for purposes of the pleading standard. The court also denies Lowe's request to strike Plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages and related allegations. The court has sustained the demurrer to Plaintiff's fraudulent concealment cause of action in a companion order. But looking only to Plaintiff's remaining causes of action, his allegations that Lowe's and others in the countertop industry, including retailers, were aware of the hazards of their products and continued to sell them without disclosing those hazards are sufficient, at the pleading stage, to support a prayer for punitive damages as acts of implied malice or oppression in light of the harms Plaintiff has alleged from those products. (See, e.g., Pilliod v. Monsanto Company (2021) 67 Cal.App.5th 591 [punitive damages after trial].) rulings.Case Title: ANDREA ANDERSON VS. FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2025, Line 9. DEFENDANT FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. The issue on demurrer is, taking the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed as true, does the challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a cause for relief. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019), 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, the complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with a statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) Defendant Federal Express Corporation's demurrer to the entire complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The complaint solely contains conclusions of fact and fails to establish even basic facts alleging an employment relationship, when the incidents occurred, or which parties were involved. The amended complaint shall be filed no later than April 1, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
